compiled_state.author: RebarBotNava

comments.author: RebarBotNava
comments.body:
Forensic classification of the 17 fsck faults (store backed up: 188MB bundle + 967-ticket NDJSON export at HEAD 8c3c26f72). CLASSIFICATION (8c4f AC2): NOT version-skew — the faults persist under the current ENQUEUE_ENRICH-era binary (main 2d1524ad8, fsck shows 'ensures: 5/5 applied', no newer-than-binary WARN). They are REAL but BENIGN artifacts.

13 ORPHAN_EVENT: all benign snapshot-staleness from a distributed compaction-vs-reconciler WRITE RACE — the Jira reconciler (env_id=reconciler) writes an assignee EDIT, a human/bot compaction in a different clone snapshots 3.7s-147s later without having observed that EDIT (so its source_event_uuids omits it), then the reconciler re-emits an IDENTICAL assignee EDIT after the snapshot which is what actually drives live state. Live 'rebar show' verified correct for all 13 (the orphan's effect is a redundant duplicate). DISPOSITION: retire each orphan EDIT file (.json -> .json.retired, which fsck's is_active_event() skips) — lossless, touches no status. rebar compact is a no-op here (short-circuits on existing SNAPSHOT); fsck --repair-snapshots is the corruption path (rebuilds from full log) and MUST NOT be used.

4 MISSING_CREATE: all local-only phantom dirs (0 tracked files, not in HEAD, no structural references). 957d/9fa4/cf5d are empty dirs (interrupted-write residue). 110e has a .cache.json holding an epic 'Operation certificates — asymmetric + environment-attributed' — which is a DUPLICATE of the already-tracked open epic 6d0d (same title); the content is NOT lost. DISPOSITION: delete all 4 local phantom dirs.

ROOT-CAUSE FOLLOW-UP (separate work): the compaction-vs-reconciler race will recur; a durable fix (e.g. reconciler-aware compaction sync, or compaction refusing to snapshot within N seconds of an unobserved remote write) is out of scope for this remediation-to-zero ticket.

compiled_state.description:
Observed 2026-07-10 during the adept-hedge-stain epic close-out: `rebar fsck` from current main (0.7.1, post-change-462) reports 14 issues — MISSING_CREATE on 9fa4-e87a-a5f0-4b7c and 13 ORPHAN_EVENT ('pre-snapshot event not captured in source_event_uuids') — while also WARNing the store contains ENQUEUE_ENRICH events newer than the running binary (written by a concurrent session on unmerged code) and 'ensures: 0/5 applied'.

Band-A (A3) remediation had previously driven the live store to fsck-zero, so either (a) these are false positives from binary/store version skew (the older binary cannot interpret the newer events' effects, so replay bookkeeping looks orphaned), or (b) a genuine regression was introduced by post-A3 write activity. Do NOT run `rebar fsck --repair` from an older binary against the newer store — upgrade first (per fsck's own WARN: reconcile hosts upgrade first).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] After the ENQUEUE_ENRICH-introducing change merges to main, re-run `rebar fsck` from that binary against the live store and record the counts on this ticket.
- [ ] If findings persist: classify each (version-skew artifact vs real orphan), and remediate real ones via the A3 tooling (`fsck --repair`, no-in-flight-pass check honored).
- [ ] If findings clear: note the false-positive mechanism here and close; consider a follow-up so fsck labels skew-suspect findings when unknown-newer event types are present.
- [ ] `ensures` converged (5/5 applied) via `rebar fsck --repair` run from the current binary.
compiled_state.title: Re-audit live-store fsck once ENQUEUE_ENRICH-era code lands: 13 ORPHAN_EVENT + 1 MISSING_CREATE reported by main's binary against a newer store
